editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from John Fitzpatrick, a blacksmith. In the letter, Fitzpatrick claims to have left his watch for repair at the Pillar House on Sackville Street before the outbreak of the Easter Rising. The watch having been lost during the Easter Rising, Fitzpatrick states that he is seeking compensation for the loss. In the letter Fitzpatrick describes himself as a 'poor man' who cannot afford the loss of the watch. He outlines the cost of the watch and states that he was advised by the Pillar House to make this claim.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13656

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
N o 17 London Bridge Rd Dublin Dear Sir Just a line to let you
know I left a watch at the
Pillar house to be repaired
a while before the rebellion
and its gone through same
and I am making an application
for compensation for it
I am a poor man and cant
afford to be at the loss of it
it cost from Â£ 3.10 s to 5 Â£ when
new it was a siles' lever double
cased watch and was very
valuable
to me so I hope Dear
sir you will consider my
application you know sir
I called for my watch to
the Pillar house and they
told me to put my application
in for the loss of it
hoping Dear sir my application
will find your kind
attention I remain yours
Sincerely John Fitzpatrick
Blacksmith
